frameworks.name: Hook-Value-Close Structure
frameworks.description: Three-part framework for short-form video content that maximizes retention and conversion
frameworks.when_to_use: All short-form video content under 2 minutes across TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts
steps: Hook (0-3 seconds): Open with compelling question, surprising statistic, or dynamic visual
Value (3-45 seconds): Deliver on hook promise with clear information or emotional payoff
Close (45-60 seconds): Reinforce main point and introduce simple next step or CTA
frameworks.example: Hook: 'Stop letting your houseplants die' → Value: Show 3 specific watering techniques → Close: 'Follow for more plant care tips'

frameworks.name: Three-Act Video Structure
frameworks.description: Classic storytelling framework adapted for marketing videos to build emotional connection
frameworks.when_to_use: Longer-form content (60+ seconds), brand storytelling, product demonstrations
steps: Act 1 - Setup: Introduce customer character and establish their problem/desire
Act 2 - Confrontation: Show character attempting solutions but facing obstacles
Act 3 - Resolution: Your product/service provides the solution and transformation
frameworks.example: Nike ad showing athlete's struggle → training obstacles → triumph with Nike gear

frameworks.name: SUE Framework (Scarcity-Urgency-Exclusivity)
frameworks.description: Psychological triggers framework for driving immediate action in video ads
frameworks.when_to_use: Product launches, limited-time offers, high-consideration purchases
steps: Scarcity: Highlight limited availability or quantities
Urgency: Create time pressure with deadlines or fast-moving opportunities
Exclusivity: Make viewers feel privileged to access the offer
frameworks.example: Music festival ad showing sold-out venues → tickets selling fast → exclusive early access for followers

key_principles.explanation: Attention is won or lost in the opening 3 seconds. 80% of viewers decide whether to continue watching within this window.
key_principles.application: Open with action, surprising statistics, compelling questions, or pattern interrupts. Never start with logos or slow build-ups.

key_principles.explanation: 85% of social video is watched without sound. Videos must communicate value visually.
key_principles.application: Use bold, readable text overlays, visual storytelling, and captions. Test videos with sound off to ensure comprehension.

key_principles.explanation: Each platform has unique algorithm preferences and user behaviors that affect performance.
key_principles.application: Adapt aspect ratios (9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 16:9 for YouTube), use platform-specific trending audio, and match platform pacing expectations.

key_principles.explanation: Modern audiences expect immediate value delivery, not delayed gratification.
key_principles.application: Lead with the benefit or solution in the first 5 seconds. Cut all unnecessary setup, pauses, or filler content.

tactics.description: Use popular audio tracks to boost algorithmic reach by 40-60%
tactics.expected_result: Increased discoverability and engagement from users who previously engaged with that audio

tactics.description: Break expected patterns with unexpected visuals, statements, or actions in opening frames
tactics.expected_result: 23% higher retention rates in first 10 seconds compared to standard openings

tactics.description: Identify pain point, amplify the frustration, then present your solution
tactics.expected_result: Higher conversion rates for problem-aware audiences, particularly effective for B2B

tactics.description: Feature real customer experiences and testimonials within structured video frameworks
tactics.expected_result: 4x higher trust scores and 2.4x higher conversion rates compared to brand-created content

tactics.description: Create 3 different opening hooks for each video concept and A/B test performance
tactics.expected_result: 15-30% improvement in overall video performance by identifying optimal hooks

templates.name: Problem Hook Template
templates.template: Stop [doing frustrating behavior]. Here's why [common solution] isn't working: [3 specific reasons]. Instead, try [your solution] because [unique benefit].
templates.use_case: Educational content, product introductions, service-based businesses

templates.name: Curiosity Gap Template
templates.template: I discovered [surprising insight] after [time period/experience]. The [number] things that changed everything: [list items with visual reveals].
templates.use_case: Personal brand content, case studies, transformation stories

templates.name: Social Proof Template
templates.template: [Number] people are already [desired outcome] with [product/method]. Here's exactly how [customer name] got [specific result] in [timeframe]: [step-by-step process].

templates.name: Trend Hijack Template
templates.template: Everyone's talking about [trending topic], but here's what they're missing: [your unique angle]. This changes [specific outcome] because [compelling reason].
templates.use_case: Thought leadership, trending topic commentary, brand positioning

common_mistakes: Starting with company logos or slow brand introductions
Saving the best content for the end instead of leading with value
Creating videos longer than 90 seconds without strong retention hooks
Using generic stock footage instead of authentic, relevant visuals
Ignoring platform-specific aspect ratios and formatting requirements
Overloading videos with multiple messages instead of focusing on one clear point
Failing to include captions or text overlays for silent viewing
Using weak CTAs or no clear next step for viewers
pro_tips: Test hooks by watching your video with sound off - if the first 3 seconds don't make sense visually, recut
Use retention curve analytics to identify exact drop-off points and optimize those moments
Create 'thumb-stop' thumbnails by using high contrast, clear faces, and bold text overlays
Repurpose one piece of long-form content into 5-7 short-form videos by extracting key moments
Study your competitors' most-viewed videos and identify their hook patterns for inspiration
Use dynamic captions that appear word-by-word to increase watch time and engagement
End videos with a question or cliffhanger to boost comments and algorithmic reach
Batch film multiple videos in one session using different hooks for the same core message
